nrtdamage package¶

Submodules¶

nrtdamage.cli module¶

cli.py - Command line interface for NRTDamage.

This module provides the command line interface to estimate radiation damage in materials using the NRT model.

nrtdamage.cli.create_parser()[source]¶

Create and return the argument parser for NRTDamage.

Returns:

The configured argument parser.

Return type:

argparse.ArgumentParser

nrtdamage.cli.main()[source]¶

Main entry point for the NRTDamage CLI.

nrtdamage.cli.run_compute(args)[source]¶

Execute the compute command.

Parameters:

args (argparse.Namespace) – Parsed command line arguments.

nrtdamage.cli.run_list()[source]¶

Execute the list command.

nrtdamage.materials module¶

materials.py - Database of materials for NRT damage calculations.

This module provides displacement energies and neutron cross-sections for common nuclear and aerospace materials.

nrtdamage.materials.get_material(name)[source]¶

Retrieve material properties by name.

Parameters:

name (str) – Name of the material (case-insensitive).

Returns:

Dictionary containing material properties.

Return type:

dict

Raises:

ValueError – If the material is not found in the database.

Examples

>>> mat = get_material("iron")
>>> mat["displacement_energy"]
40.0
nrtdamage.materials.list_materials()[source]¶

Return a list of all available material names.

Returns:

Sorted list of available material names.

Return type:

list of str

Examples

>>> materials = list_materials()
>>> "iron" in materials
True

nrtdamage.physics module¶

physics.py - Core NRT damage model calculations.

This module implements the Norgett-Robinson-Torrens (NRT) model for estimating atomic displacement damage in irradiated materials.

nrtdamage.physics.compute_dpa(flux, cross_section, damage_energy, displacement_energy, time)[source]¶

Calculate the total DPA (displacements per atom).

Computes the accumulated radiation damage in a material exposed to a neutron flux over a given time period.

Parameters:

  • flux (float) – Neutron flux in n/cm²/s. Must be positive.

  • cross_section (float) – Neutron cross-section of the material in cm². Must be positive.

  • damage_energy (float) – Average damage energy per collision in eV. Must be positive.

  • displacement_energy (float) – Threshold displacement energy of the material in eV. Must be positive.

  • time (float) – Irradiation time in seconds. Must be positive.

Returns:

The total DPA value (dimensionless).

Return type:

float

Raises:

ValueError – If any parameter is not positive.

Examples

>>> compute_dpa(1e14, 1e-24, 1000.0, 40.0, 3.15e7)
0.0025199999999999997
nrtdamage.physics.nrt_displacements(damage_energy, displacement_energy)[source]¶

Calculate the number of displaced atoms using the NRT model.

The NRT model estimates the number of Frenkel pairs (vacancies and interstitials) produced when a Primary Knock-on Atom (PKA) receives a given damage energy. This is the standard model used in nuclear materials science.

Parameters:

  • damage_energy (float) – The damage energy deposited in the material, in eV. Must be positive.

  • displacement_energy (float) – The threshold displacement energy of the material, in eV. Must be positive.

Returns:

The estimated number of displaced atoms (dimensionless).

Return type:

float

Raises:

ValueError – If damage_energy or displacement_energy are not positive.

Examples

>>> nrt_displacements(1000.0, 40.0)
10.0

nrtdamage.plots module¶

plots.py - Visualization functions for NRT damage results.

This module provides plotting functions to visualize radiation damage as a function of various parameters.

Note: visualization functions are not tested as per project guidelines.

nrtdamage.plots.plot_dpa_comparison(flux, damage_energy=1000.0, time_years=1.0, save_path=None)[source]¶

Plot DPA comparison across all available materials.

Parameters:

  • flux (float) – Neutron flux in n/cm2/s.

  • damage_energy (float, optional) – Average damage energy per collision in eV. Default is 1000.0.

  • time_years (float, optional) – Irradiation time in years. Default is 1.0.

  • save_path (str or None, optional) – If provided, saves the plot to this path. Default is None.

nrtdamage.plots.plot_dpa_vs_time(material_name, flux, damage_energy=1000.0, max_time_years=10, save_path=None)[source]¶

Plot DPA accumulation over time for a given material.

Parameters:

  • material_name (str) – Name of the material to plot.

  • flux (float) – Neutron flux in n/cm2/s.

  • damage_energy (float, optional) – Average damage energy per collision in eV. Default is 1000.0.

  • max_time_years (float, optional) – Maximum irradiation time in years. Default is 10.

  • save_path (str or None, optional) – If provided, saves the plot to this path. Default is None.

Module contents¶

nrtdamage - A Python tool to estimate radiation damage in materials using the NRT (Norgett-Robinson-Torrens) model.

nrtdamage.compute_dpa(flux, cross_section, damage_energy, displacement_energy, time)[source]¶

Calculate the total DPA (displacements per atom).

Computes the accumulated radiation damage in a material exposed to a neutron flux over a given time period.

Parameters:

  • flux (float) – Neutron flux in n/cm²/s. Must be positive.

  • cross_section (float) – Neutron cross-section of the material in cm². Must be positive.

  • damage_energy (float) – Average damage energy per collision in eV. Must be positive.

  • displacement_energy (float) – Threshold displacement energy of the material in eV. Must be positive.

  • time (float) – Irradiation time in seconds. Must be positive.

Returns:

The total DPA value (dimensionless).

Return type:

float

Raises:

ValueError – If any parameter is not positive.

Examples

>>> compute_dpa(1e14, 1e-24, 1000.0, 40.0, 3.15e7)
0.0025199999999999997
nrtdamage.get_material(name)[source]¶

Retrieve material properties by name.

Parameters:

name (str) – Name of the material (case-insensitive).

Returns:

Dictionary containing material properties.

Return type:

dict

Raises:

ValueError – If the material is not found in the database.

Examples

>>> mat = get_material("iron")
>>> mat["displacement_energy"]
40.0
nrtdamage.list_materials()[source]¶

Return a list of all available material names.

Returns:

Sorted list of available material names.

Return type:

list of str

Examples

>>> materials = list_materials()
>>> "iron" in materials
True
nrtdamage.nrt_displacements(damage_energy, displacement_energy)[source]¶

Calculate the number of displaced atoms using the NRT model.

The NRT model estimates the number of Frenkel pairs (vacancies and interstitials) produced when a Primary Knock-on Atom (PKA) receives a given damage energy. This is the standard model used in nuclear materials science.

Parameters:

  • damage_energy (float) – The damage energy deposited in the material, in eV. Must be positive.

  • displacement_energy (float) – The threshold displacement energy of the material, in eV. Must be positive.

Returns:

The estimated number of displaced atoms (dimensionless).

Return type:

float

Raises:

ValueError – If damage_energy or displacement_energy are not positive.

Examples

>>> nrt_displacements(1000.0, 40.0)
10.0

NRTDamage
